theory.concept:
WHERE filters rows from a table based on a condition — instead of getting every row back, you only get the ones where the condition is true.
• Works the same whether you use SELECT * or specific columns — WHERE filters rows, not columns
• Text values need quotes ('Kolkata'), numbers don't
• Combine conditions with AND (all must be true) or OR (at least one must be true)
• Comparison operators: =, != (or <>), <, >, <=, >=
theory.syntax:
SELECT * FROM table_name WHERE condition;

SELECT column1, column2 FROM table_name WHERE condition1 AND condition2;

shared_table.name: Students
shared_table.create_sql: CREATE TABLE Students (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, name TEXT, age INTEGER, city TEXT, marks INTEGER);
shared_table.insert_sql: INSERT INTO Students (id, name, age, city, marks) VALUES (1, 'Aditya', 21, 'Kolkata', 78), (2, 'Priya', 22, 'Delhi', 85), (3, 'Rohan', 20, 'Mumbai', 62), (4, 'Sneha', 23, 'Kolkata', 91), (5, 'Karan', 21, 'Bangalore', 55), (6, 'Anjali', 22, 'Delhi', 73), (7, 'Vikram', 20, 'Chennai', 88), (8, 'Meera', 23, 'Mumbai', 67);

questions.prompt: Select the names of students who scored more than 75 marks.
Select the name and marks of students from 'Kolkata' or 'Mumbai'.
Select the name, age, and city of students who are exactly 21 years old and scored below 60.
